audit_items.proof_trace: In nfp_reprs_resync_phys_ports (L535), when a physical port transitions to NFP_PORT_INVALID, only nfp_repr_clean(repr) is called instead of nfp_repr_clean_and_free(repr). This omits __nfp_repr_free(repr) (L411-415), which is the only code that calls free_percpu(repr->stats) and free_netdev(repr->netdev). No priv_destructor is set on the netdev, so the kernel networking layer will not free repr->stats automatically after unregister_netdev, leaving per-CPU stats memory permanently leaked.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An entity capable of triggering repeated physical port invalidation events (e.g., via hardware hot-plug, SFP removal cycles, or firmware-driven port state changes) causes nfp_reprs_resync_phys_ports to leak per-CPU stats allocations on each cycle, progressively exhausting kernel memory and degrading system stability.
